derivation_basis: Brazil sample window spans high/transition fertility in the 1980s and near/below-replacement fertility by the 2010s.
Namibia remains above replacement during the verified 1990-2012 study window; this is a late first-transition setting.
Italy is already a low-fertility, below-replacement setting during the verified 1998-2004 analysis window.
China is below replacement during 2012-2020, but the one-child/two-child-policy environment means SDT interpretation should be treated cautiously.
Prussia 1881-1910 falls in the classic first demographic transition window.
China is below replacement during 2010-2014, but fertility is policy-constrained; SDT interpretation should be treated cautiously.
China is below replacement during 2010-2018; this row is mechanism evidence with a non-fertility outcome, so target-period relevance is contextual.
Imperial Germany 1895-1907 is a first-demographic-transition setting.
United States 1850 is pre-1870 and directly adjacent to the nineteenth-century fertility transition.
United States 1940-1960 is before the project SDT boundary and sits within the late first-transition/baby-boom period.
Germany is below replacement throughout the 1984-2017 SOEP study window, so this is an SDT setting under the replacement-status rule.
The Netherlands is below replacement throughout the relevant 2004-2021 pre-reform/control and outcome window, so this is an SDT setting.
Australia is below replacement throughout the 2001-2022 HILDA window, so this is an SDT setting.
